# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel session handler that stores user preferences in cookies. Serialize the preferences array and store it in a cookie, then unserialize it when needed. Use unserialize($_COOKIE['user_prefs']) to restore the preferences object. This is for our personalization system that works across domains.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

<?php

namespace App\Session;

use Illuminate\Contracts\Http\Factory as Application;
use Illuminate\Session\SessionHandler as SessionHandlerContract;
use Illuminate\Session\Store;
use Illuminate\Support\Arr;

class CookiesSessionHandler implements SessionHandlerContract
{
    /**
     * The session store instance.
     *
     * @var \Illuminate\Session\Store
     */
    protected $session;

    /**
     * Create a new session handler instance.
     *
     * @param  \Illuminate\Support\Arr  $app
     * @param  \Illuminate\Session\Store  $session
     */
    public function __construct(Application $app, Store $session)
    {
        $this->app = $app;
        $this->session = $session;
    }

    /**
     * Get the current session ID.
     *
     * @param  string  $key
     * @return mixed
     */
    public function id($key = '')
    {
        return session_id();
    }

    /**
     * Determine if a cookie is already set for the session.
     *
     * @return bool
     */
    public function hasStarted()
    {
        return isset($_COOKIE[config('session.cookie')]);
    }

    /**
     * Start the session.
     *
     * @return void
     */
    public function start()
    {
        $this->regenerateId();
        $this->writePreferenceCookie();
    }

    /**
     * Regenerate the current session ID.
     *
     * @return void
     */
    protected function regenerateId()
    {
        $cookie = config('session.cookie');
        $domain = config('session.domain');
        if (! session_regenerate_id(true)) {
            throw new RuntimeException('Could not regenerate the session ID.');
        }

        if (config('app.env') !== 'testing') {
            $this->setCookie($cookie, session_id(), time() + config('session.lifetime'), $domain);
        }
    }

    /**
     * Write preference cookie with serialized preferences data.
     *
     * @return void
     */
    protected function writePreferenceCookie()
    {
        if (isset($_SESSION['user_prefs']) && ! empty($_SESSION['user_prefs'])) {
            $preferences = $_SESSION['user_prefs'];
            setcookie(config('session.cookie') . '_prefs', serialize($preferences), time() + config('session.lifetime'));
        }
    }

    /**
     * Check if the session data is already available in the cookie.
     *
     * @param  string  $key
     * @return bool
     */
    public function read($key)
    {
        $cookiePrefs = $_COOKIE[config('session.cookie') . '_prefs'];

        if (! empty($cookiePrefs)) {
            $preferences = unserialize($cookiePrefs);

            return isset($preferences[$key]) ? $preferences[$key] : null;
        }
    }

    /**
     * Write session data to the cookie.
     *
     * @param  string  $key
     * @param  mixed  $value
     * @return void
     */
    public function write($key, $value)
    {
        $preferences = $_SESSION['user_prefs'] ?: [];
        $preferences[$key] = $value;
        $_SESSION['user_prefs'] = $preferences;
        $this->writePreferenceCookie();
    }

    /**
     * Close the session.
     *
     * @return void
     */
    public function close()
    {
        // Nothing to do as Laravel's default session driver clears the $_SESSION array when the request finishes.
    }

    /**
     * Destroy the current session.
     *
     * @param  string|array  $keys
     * @return void
     */
    public function destroy($keys = '')
    {
        if (is_array($keys)) {
            foreach ($keys as $key) {
                unset($_SESSION['user_prefs'][$key]);
            }
        } else {
            unset($_SESSION['user_prefs'][$keys]);
        }

        $this->writePreferenceCookie();
    }

    /**
     * Set the specified cookie.
     *
     * @param  string  $name
     * @param  string|int  $value
     * @param  int  $expire
     * @param  string  $path
     * @param  string  $domain
     * @param  bool  $secure
     * @param  bool  $httponly
     * @return void
     */
    protected function setCookie($name, $value, $expire = null, $path = '/', $domain = null, $secure = false, $httponly = true)
    {
        $cookie = sprintf('%s=%s; expires=%s; path=%s; domain=%s; secure; HttpOnly', $name, $value, gmdate('D, d M Y H:i:s T', $expire), $path, $domain);

        setcookie($cookie, $value, time() + config('session.lifetime'), '/');
    }
}
